title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost-management SaaS trusted by The New York Times, Wiz, and Lyft, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations reporting to the CRO. The role requires 7+ years in RevOps/Sales Ops within high-growth B2B SaaS, with proven experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR. Candidates need deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance, and HR, and experience collaborating across time zones including the Tel Aviv HQ. A hands-on leader-doer mindset is essential.
score_reason: Excellent match across all dimensions: 15+ years exceeds the 7+ year requirement, deep Salesforce expertise aligns perfectly, B2B SaaS and Fintech background match the company profile, and the Tel Aviv hybrid setup is ideal for his location preferences. Head of RevOps is his target role at a high-growth, well-funded company.
ai_opener: Omri, I came across Finout's Head of Revenue Operations role and immediately thought of your background. With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS RevOps and deep Salesforce expertise, you've likely encountered the exact scaling challenges Finout is solving for enterprises like The New York Times and Wiz—and the Tel Aviv/NY hybrid setup seems built for your profile. I'd love to explore whether this Head of RevOps role aligns with your next move.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong, the revenue intelligence platform, is hiring a RevOps Manager focused on Territory Strategy & Operations to accelerate revenue growth and seller productivity through data-driven territory design. Requires 3–5+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Strategy, or GTM planning, deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models and segmentation, strong analytical skills (Excel, SQL, BI tools like Tableau/Retool), and Salesforce proficiency. Territory management tools experience (Fullcast, Pigment, Anaplan) is a plus. Candidates must be within commuting distance of a hub location.
score_reason: Excellent match: 15+ years B2B SaaS RevOps experience exceeds 3-5 year requirement, strong Salesforce proficiency, deep GTM/pipeline forecasting expertise directly applicable to territory strategy and seller productivity optimization. Territory management tools experience would be a bonus acquisition.
ai_opener: Omri, I noticed your deep expertise in pipeline forecasting and GTM strategy at scale—exactly what Gong needs to optimize their territory design and seller productivity. Gong's revenue intelligence platform is in a unique position to leverage data-driven territory segmentation, and your B2B SaaS RevOps background would directly accelerate their growth motion. I think you'd find their Territory Strategy & Operations challenge compelling.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Conveo, an AI research platform used by Unilever, Google, and Orange, is hiring a GTM Engineer to build internal tools and automations that make its GTM team highly effective. As the company scales 20x in revenue, the role focuses on replacing manual workflows with purpose-built apps, pipelines, and AI-powered workflows—including lead scoring models that learn, attribution pipelines, faster onboarding flows, and AI agents that eliminate hours of manual work weekly. Candidates must be able to code, ship, and iterate quickly.
score_reason: Strong alignment on GTM/RevOps expertise and AI-native tooling, but the role heavily emphasizes coding/shipping technical products, which may be outside Omri's typical wheelhouse as an executive. Best fit if he has proven hands-on engineering chops beyond traditional RevOps.
ai_opener: Conveo's 20x scaling mission resonates—I've built similar AI-powered GTM stacks at scale (lead scoring, attribution, workflow automation). Your focus on replacing manual work with purpose-built apps aligns with how I think about RevOps in 2024. I'd be curious whether you're looking for someone who codes full-time or a GTM ops leader who can architect and partner with engineering.

title: GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: Nooks, the AI Sales Assistant Platform backed by Kleiner Perkins with $70M+ raised, is hiring its first GTM Engineer to serve as a technical co-pilot for the Sales Development organization. Responsibilities include owning setup, optimization, and enablement within the Nooks platform, identifying and resolving process bottlenecks and data issues. Requires 2+ years of sales development experience selling into sales leaders, B2B SaaS prospecting experience, strong analytic skills, and familiarity with Nooks, HubSpot, ZoomInfo, and Sales Navigator.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, RevOps expertise, and AI-native platform focus, but the role emphasizes SDR management and sales development experience over Omri's broader revenue operations and pipeline forecasting background. The 2+ years SDR requirement is below his seniority level, suggesting potential overqualification or role mismatch.
ai_opener: I saw Nooks is building the first GTM Engineer role to optimize your sales development engine—given your KP backing and AI-native platform, you need someone who can architect RevOps workflows across HubSpot, prospecting tools, and your platform ecosystem. With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM and deep experience in sales pipeline optimization and data integrity, I can help you move beyond manual enablement into predictive SDR productivity systems.

title: GTM Engineer
description: DISQO is hiring a GTM Engineer with 3–5+ years in Sales, Marketing, Operations, or GTM roles, preferably B2B. The role requires demonstrated success deploying GenAI and workflow tools across GTM (lead scoring, intent signals, outbound triggers, AI briefs), strong data literacy and understanding of data models in tools like Salesforce and HubSpot, and cross-functional operator skills. Candidates should be nimble and scrappy, comfortable launching MVPs and iterating. Experience with workflow automation tools like Clay or n8n and building agents (Gemini Enterprise) is valued.
score_reason: Strong alignment with Omri's RevOps/GTM expertise, AI-native GTM focus, and deep Salesforce/HubSpot knowledge. The GTM Engineer role matches his senior level perfectly, though the 3-5 year requirement is slightly junior relative to his 15+ year background—he'd be overqualified but this is offset by the AI-native and workflow automation demands where he can add immediate value.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building GTM engines at scale in B2B SaaS, and DISQO's GenAI-first approach to lead scoring, intent signals, and workflow automation is exactly the evolution I'm watching. I've deployed similar AI-driven systems across Salesforce and HubSpot stacks and would love to explore how we could architect DISQO's GTM stack to turn signals into predictable revenue faster.

title: Head of Growth - SaaS B2B
description: efficiently, a SaaS platform for design and construction teams, is hiring a Head of Growth Marketing reporting directly to the CEO to build and scale its B2B growth engine. The role owns demand generation, pipeline creation, and multi-channel campaigns (LinkedIn, Google, Meta, email, events), funnel optimization, and the MarTech stack (Salesforce, Klaviyo, Instantly, GA4). Requires B2B SaaS experience, a proven track record scaling multi-channel campaigns tied to revenue, strong marketing automation/CRM knowledge, a data-driven attribution mindset, and a builder's approach to systems and playbooks.
score_reason: Strong alignment on B2B SaaS, Salesforce expertise, and data-driven GTM mindset. However, this is a marketing operations/demand gen role rather than core RevOps/revenue operations, and the heavy emphasis on media buying (LinkedIn, Google, Meta) and marketing automation (Klaviyo, Instantly) sits outside Omri's primary wheelhouse of pipeline forecasting, sales operations, and revenue infrastructure.

With 15+ years building revenue infrastructure and GTM systems in B2B SaaS, I've spent considerable time architecting the demand gen → pipeline → forecast workflows that connect marketing's metrics to board-level revenue metrics. I'm curious if you're looking for someone equally comfortable owning the revenue operations side of that flywheel.
